artifacts.summary: Mathematical analysis of BFMCS temporal coherence blocker with recommended FMP-based completeness path

next_steps: Run /plan 982 to create implementation plan based on FMP completeness strategy

key_findings: W/D separation is necessary but not sufficient for temporal coherence
forward_F/backward_P require witnesses IN the FMCS domain D, not just in W
The m > 2k edge case is a manifestation of the F-persistence dead end
FMP-based completeness avoids BFMCS temporal coherence requirements
Dense validity implies finite validity via order embedding
Modal saturation should be handled separately via multi-family BFMCS
metadata.recommended_path: FMP-based completeness (Option B) combined with modal saturation (Option D)
